Nest application successfully started|listening on|Application is running

allow: Bash(rm /Users/lukafurlan/Documents/aio-bloggy/backend/prisma/prisma.config.ts && npm uninstall @prisma/client prisma && npm install @prisma/client@5 && npm install -D prisma@5)

Bash(# First register a user with a known email
curl -s -X POST http://localhost:3000/auth/register \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"email":"duplicate@example.com","password":"password123","name":"Duplicate User"}' > /dev/null 2>&1

# Now try to register again with the same email
curl -s -X POST http://localhost:3000/auth/register \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"email":"duplicate@example.com","password":"password123","name":"Duplicate User 2"}' \
  -w "\n%{http_code}")

Bash(# First register a user for login testing
curl -s -X POST http://localhost:3000/auth/register \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"email":"logintest@example.com","password":"password123","name":"Login Test User"}' > /dev/null 2>&1

# Now login with those credentials
curl -s -X POST http://localhost:3000/auth/login \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"email":"logintest@example.com","password":"password123"}' \
  -w "\n%{http_code}")

Bash(# Get a fresh token
TOKEN=$(curl -s -X POST http://localhost:3000/auth/login \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"email":"logintest@example.com","password":"password123"}' | grep -o '"token":"[^"]*"' | cut -d'"' -f4)

# Use the token to get user data
curl -s http://localhost:3000/auth/me \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -w "\n%{http_code}")

Bash(# First create a post to retrieve
TOKEN=$(curl -s -X POST http://localhost:3000/auth/login \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"email":"logintest@example.com","password":"password123"}' | grep -o '"token":"[^"]*"' | cut -d'"' -f4)

# Create a post
POST_RESPONSE=$(curl -s -X POST http://localhost:3000/posts \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -d '{"title":"Test Post for GET","content":"This is a test post content for retrieval testing"}')

POST_ID=$(echo $POST_RESPONSE | grep -o '"id":[0-9]*' | head -1 | cut -d':' -f2)

# Now get the post
curl -s "http://localhost:3000/posts/$POST_ID" -w "\n%{http_code}")

Bash(# Get a fresh token
TOKEN=$(curl -s -X POST http://localhost:3000/auth/login \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"email":"logintest@example.com","password":"password123"}' | grep -o '"token":"[^"]*"' | cut -d'"' -f4)

echo "Token: $TOKEN"

# Create a post and capture response
echo "Creating post..."
POST_RESPONSE=$(curl -s -X POST http://localhost:3000/posts \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -d '{"title":"Test Post for GET","content":"This is a test post content for retrieval testing"}')

echo "Post response: $POST_RESPONSE"

# Extract ID
POST_ID=$(echo $POST_RESPONSE | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['id'])" 2>/dev/null)
echo "Post ID: $POST_ID"

# Now get the post
echo "Getting post..."
curl -s "http://localhost:3000/posts/$POST_ID" -w "\nHTTP_STATUS: %{http_code}")

Bash(# Test the post creation with verbose output
TOKEN=$(curl -s -X POST http://localhost:3000/auth/login \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"email":"logintest@example.com","password":"password123"}' | grep -o '"token":"[^"]*"' | cut -d'"' -f4)

curl -v -X POST http://localhost:3000/posts \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -d '{"title":"Test Post","content":"Test content"}' 2>&1)

Bash(TIMESTAMP=$(date +%s)

# Register first test user
USER1_RESPONSE=$(curl -s -X POST http://localhost:3000/auth/register \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d "{\"email\":\"testuser_${TIMESTAMP}@example.com\",\"password\":\"password123\",\"name\":\"Test User\"}")

echo "User 1 Response: $USER1_RESPONSE"

# Extract token from user 1
USER1_TOKEN=$(echo "$USER1_RESPONSE" | grep -o '"access_token":"[^"]*"' | cut -d'"' -f4)
echo "User 1 Token: $USER1_TOKEN"

# Register second test user
USER2_RESPONSE=$(curl -s -X POST http://localhost:3000/auth/register \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d "{\"email\":\"otheruser_${TIMESTAMP}@example.com\",\"password\":\"password123\",\"name\":\"Other User\"}")

echo "User 2 Response: $USER2_RESPONSE"

# Extract token from user 2
USER2_TOKEN=$(echo "$USER2_RESPONSE" | grep -o '"access_token":"[^"]*"' | cut -d'"' -f4)
echo "User 2 Token: $USER2_TOKEN"

# Create initial post with user 1
POST_RESPONSE=$(curl -s -X POST http://localhost:3000/posts \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $USER1_TOKEN" \
  -d '{"title":"Setup Post","content":"This is a setup post for testing"}')

echo "Post Response: $POST_RESPONSE"

# Extract post ID
POST_ID=$(echo "$POST_RESPONSE" | grep -o '"id":"[^"]*"' | head -1 | cut -d'"' -f4)
echo "Post ID: $POST_ID"

# Save for later use
echo "USER1_TOKEN=$USER1_TOKEN" > /tmp/e2e_test_vars.sh
echo "USER2_TOKEN=$USER2_TOKEN" >> /tmp/e2e_test_vars.sh
echo "POST_ID=$POST_ID" >> /tmp/e2e_test_vars.sh)

Bash(TIMESTAMP=$(date +%s)

# Register first test user
USER1_RESPONSE=$(curl -s -X POST http://localhost:3000/auth/register \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d "{\"email\":\"testuser_${TIMESTAMP}@example.com\",\"password\":\"password123\",\"name\":\"Test User\"}")

echo "User 1 Response: $USER1_RESPONSE"

# Extract token from user 1 (field is "token" not "access_token")
USER1_TOKEN=$(echo "$USER1_RESPONSE" | grep -o '"token":"[^"]*"' | cut -d'"' -f4)
echo "User 1 Token: $USER1_TOKEN"

# Register second test user
USER2_RESPONSE=$(curl -s -X POST http://localhost:3000/auth/register \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d "{\"email\":\"otheruser_${TIMESTAMP}@example.com\",\"password\":\"password123\",\"name\":\"Other User\"}")

echo "User 2 Response: $USER2_RESPONSE"

# Extract token from user 2
USER2_TOKEN=$(echo "$USER2_RESPONSE" | grep -o '"token":"[^"]*"' | cut -d'"' -f4)
echo "User 2 Token: $USER2_TOKEN"

# Create initial post with user 1
POST_RESPONSE=$(curl -s -X POST http://localhost:3000/posts \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $USER1_TOKEN" \
  -d '{"title":"Setup Post","content":"This is a setup post for testing"}')

echo "Post Response: $POST_RESPONSE"

# Extract post ID
POST_ID=$(echo "$POST_RESPONSE" | grep -o '"id":"[^"]*"' | head -1 | cut -d'"' -f4)
echo "Post ID: $POST_ID"

# Save for later use
echo "USER1_TOKEN=$USER1_TOKEN" > /tmp/e2e_test_vars.sh
echo "USER2_TOKEN=$USER2_TOKEN" >> /tmp/e2e_test_vars.sh
echo "POST_ID=$POST_ID" >> /tmp/e2e_test_vars.sh

cat /tmp/e2e_test_vars.sh)

Bash(# The post response has numeric ID, let me extract it correctly
POST_RESPONSE='{"id":1,"title":"Setup Post","content":"This is a setup post for testing","published":true,"createdAt":"2026-01-26T08:41:19.087Z","updatedAt":"2026-01-26T08:41:19.087Z","authorId":6,"author":{"id":6,"name":"Test User"}}'

# Extract numeric ID
POST_ID=$(echo "$POST_RESPONSE" | grep -o '"id":[0-9]*' | head -1 | cut -d':' -f2)
echo "Post ID: $POST_ID"

# Update the file
source /tmp/e2e_test_vars.sh
echo "USER1_TOKEN=$USER1_TOKEN" > /tmp/e2e_test_vars.sh
echo "USER2_TOKEN=$USER2_TOKEN" >> /tmp/e2e_test_vars.sh
echo "POST_ID=1" >> /tmp/e2e_test_vars.sh

cat /tmp/e2e_test_vars.sh)

Bash(HTTP_CODE=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" -X POST http://localhost:3000/posts \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"title":"Test","content":"Test content here"}')

echo "HTTP Code: $HTTP_CODE"

if [ "$HTTP_CODE" = "401" ]; then
  echo "PASS: Returns 401 without auth"
else
  echo "FAIL: Expected 401, got $HTTP_CODE"
fi)
